action.content: Can you write a poem about the pros and cons of playing a lot of video games? Please make sure it's at least 40 sentences long (don't forget to add punctuations). You must highlight some words or phrases in your response, like *highlighted phrase*.
action.label: context

action.content:
Video games offer a world where **magic happens** at the touch of a button.
You can **fly through the sky** without ever needing to stretch your legs.
The graphics are **stunning and bright**, captivating your attention completely.
You feel a **sense of achievement** when you finally beat a tough boss.
Your **reaction times improve** as you react to threats instantly.
You can **travel anywhere** in the game, escaping the limits of your room.
Socially, they provide a way to **connect with friends** from all over the globe.
Playing together can create **strong bonds** that last for years.
The stories are **engaging and deep**, often rivaling real novels.
You can **relax after a long day** by sinking into your chair.
They offer a **safe space** to experiment with ideas you cannot try in real life.
You can **master complex skills** like strategy and quick decision-making.
The music often **calms your mind** or pumps up your energy.
Some games teach **history and culture** in an interactive format.
They can **boost your brain power** by solving puzzles and logic problems.
You might **discover hidden secrets** that make you feel like a detective.
There is a **sense of control** that comes from commanding a virtual army.
The **progression system** lets you see how you grow stronger with every level.
You can **customize your avatar** to express your unique style.
The **challenge** keeps your mind sharp and focused on a single goal.
Sometimes the **adrenaline rush** feels just like being in an action movie.
You can **explore vast landscapes** that no one has ever seen in reality.
The **storyline** often twists and turns, keeping you on the edge of your seat.
It is **fun to retry** a level until you finally get it right.
You feel **empowered** when your efforts lead to a successful outcome.
The **competition** can motivate you to push past your own limits.
However, too much **screen time** can hurt your eyes over time.
Your **sleep schedule** may get disrupted if you stay up too late.
You might **forget to eat** or drink water while staring at the screen.
**Physical activity** often gets replaced by hours of sitting still.
You could **neglect your real friends** in favor of online companions.
The **cost of hardware** and games can put a strain on your budget.
There is a risk of **losing track of time** and forgetting responsibilities.
**Mood swings** can happen if you lose a game or face failure.
Some games contain **violence** that might be disturbing for young minds.
You might develop **addictive habits** that interfere with daily life.
The **reality vs. virtual world** confusion can make it hard to distinguish the two.
Your **posture and back** can suffer from hours of hunched sitting.
You might **avoid social situations** where you have to talk to people directly.
The **dopamine hits** from rewards can make real life feel boring by comparison.
It is easy to **procrastinate** on schoolwork or chores when gaming is so tempting.
You might **miss out** on real-world experiences that cannot be digitized.
The **pressure to win** can cause unnecessary stress and anxiety.
Sometimes the **gameplay loops** feel repetitive and dull after a while.
You might **isolate yourself** from the community around you in real life.
Despite the **drawbacks**, many find balance with moderate play.
The key is to **know when to stop** before the fun turns into trouble.
Ultimately, games are **tools** that can enhance or hinder your life depending on use.
You must **choose wisely** how much time you spend in the virtual realm.
Playing in **moderation** allows you to enjoy the benefits without the cons.
So remember to **put the controller down** when your real life needs you most.
action.label: response
